steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The adjective "gorgeous" directly modifies "display," indicating the word must mean something visually impressive or striking.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The word "display" needs to mean a visually striking sight — Mr. Bradford was conscious of this gorgeous ______ of nature around him. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Spectacle: this gorgeous spectacle — spectacle means a visually impressive or striking scene, fitting perfectly with the lush, vivid natural scenery described (moss, pine green, cypresses, willows). ✓
  • Choice B — Pretentiousness: this gorgeous pretentiousness — pretentiousness means an exaggerated claim to importance; nature cannot be pretentious, and the passage presents the scenery admiringly, not critically. ✗
  • Choice C — Resemblance: this gorgeous resemblance — resemblance means a likeness or similarity between things; the passage describes the scene itself, not its similarity to something else. ✗
  • Choice D — Deception: this gorgeous deception — deception means a trick or false impression; nothing in the passage suggests the natural scenery is misleading or illusory. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
